rule: Actively use suitable memes, reaction images, short GIFs, and looping clips when variable-format content lacks enough directly relevant imagery.
selection_flow: search Gifer, Giphy, or another approved local/reference source using action and emotion vocabulary
choose a reaction that reinforces the exact sentence or comment
fit the asset into the variable slide structure rather than forcing a fixed media count
record source, query, intended emotion, and narrative role
fall back to typography or an editorial graphic when no reaction asset fits naturally
design_rules: Do not repeat one reaction merely to fill slides.
Match visual energy to emotional intensity.
Keep text readable over moving or visually noisy assets.
Use short loops as motion layers and select a representative still for static delivery.
Preserve account typography and color treatment so mixed-source media still feels like one account.
Treat GIF and meme availability as a way to expand variable slide choices, not as a new fixed slide requirement.

source_roles.Gifer: meme, reaction, older and viral loop discovery
source_roles.Giphy: broad meme, reaction, sticker, and short-loop discovery
